channel.title: Optimization on Tom M. Ragonneau
channel.description: Recent content in Optimization on Tom M. Ragonneau

item.title: COBYQA
item.description: COBYQA (Constrained Optimization BY Quadratic Approximations) is a derivative-free trust-region SQP optimization solver for constrained optimization using quadratic approximations. It is currently implemented in Python.

item.title: PDFO
item.description: PDFO (Powell’s Derivative-Free Optimization solvers) is a cross-platform package providing MATLAB and Python interfaces for using late Professor M. J. D. Powell’s derivative-free optimization solvers. It includes UOBYQA, NEWUOA, BOBYQA, LINCOA, and COBYLA.

item.title: Kantorovich inequality
item.description: Let $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ be a given symmetric positive definite matrix and denote its eigenvalues $\lambda_1 \le \lambda_2 \le \dots \le \lambda_n$. The Kantorovich inequality states that for all vector $x \in \mathbb{R}^n$ of unit norm, we have $$ \langle x, A x \rangle \langle x, A^{-1} x \rangle \le \frac{(\lambda_1 + \lambda_n)^2}{4 \lambda_1 \lambda_n}.
